question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks essentially zero didactic questions. Turn 1 is a full lecture dumping all three approaches with code. Turn 2 asks logistical clarifying questions (language? which approach? tests?) which the Pair then ignores by just writing all three. No teachable-moment probes occur.

question_quality.rationale: The only questions are cosmetic logistics (pick a language, pick an approach). They don't surface any algorithmic crux — the shorter-side-binding insight, the off-by-one on equality, the no-need-for-max(0,...) guard — all of these are just narrated, never asked.

scaffolding.rationale: There is effectively no scaffolding. The Pair front-loads the optimal solution plus two alternatives and a complexity table in Turn 1, spoiling everything. Subsequent turns just write the file and run tests; no graded difficulty or guided discovery.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and well-formatted, but flow is one-directional: the Pair monologues, the user (acting as a 'pair') tries to redirect, and the Pair brushes past with 'we already have all three implementations above.' The conversation is the Pair lecturing rather than collaborating.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code correctly solves the problem with all three approaches and 11/11 tests passing, comparable to the baseline's working solution.

friction.rationale: Shorter than baseline (2 turns / 104s vs. 5 turns / 147s) but the brevity comes from skipping pedagogy rather than tightening it. Roughly comparable productivity, no proportional teaching payoff for the time saved.
